description: A clean designed bar-chart infographic from the U.S. Department of Labor on a white background, framed in DOL's signature navy and light-blue palette with a serif typeface. The headline at top reads 'AVERAGE SALARY AFTER PROGRAM COMPLETION' with an italic subtitle 'For recent college and apprenticeship graduates, rounded to nearest thousand.' Two vertical bars compare earnings: a shorter light-blue bar labeled 'COLLEGE GRADUATES' topped with '$69K', and a taller dark-navy bar labeled 'APPRENTICES' topped with '$80K'. The y-axis is scaled 25K/50K/75K/100K. A small DOL agency seal sits in the lower-left corner, and a source line at bottom-left reads 'Source: CNBC and Apprenticeship.gov'. The propaganda/messaging technique is favorable statistical framing: it visually argues that apprenticeships (skilled trades) out-earn four-year college degrees, supporting the administration's 'learn a trade, get paid' / skilled-labor recruitment push. Target audience is young people and parents weighing college versus the trades.
notable_text: AVERAGE SALARY AFTER PROGRAM COMPLETION; COLLEGE GRADUATES $69K; APPRENTICES $80K; Source: CNBC and Apprenticeship.gov
